title: Lap-Group Count Plus Within-Lap Reranker
summary: Pit calls are not independent: teams react to opponents on the same race lap.
rationale: Pit calls are not independent: teams react to opponents on the same race lap. Learning-to-rank methods are built for grouped decisions, and F1 strategy papers emphasize competitive interaction rather than isolated per-row classification.
implementation_hint: For each (Year,Race,LapNumber), train a small count model for how many cars pit next lap, then a ranker with qid=(Year,Race,LapNumber) to order drivers within that lap-group. Convert ranks to probabilities by matching the predicted pit-count or blending with the current classifier.
expected_effect: Should improve ordering on busy strategy windows where several cars are simultaneously in-range, lifting AUC beyond a row-wise-only model.
risk: If many lap-groups have zero or one positive, the ranker may be noisy unless regularized and blended with the base classifier.
